name: Squarespace
price_basis: Core plan at $29 per month, annual billing prices as displayed, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: TRIM: keep the site, drop Core to Basic. Zo covers the business features that pushed you up a tier.
what_zo_builds: The gap between Basic and Core, not the website. Zo builds the deeper analytics report Core charges for as a weekly automation over your site exports, hosts lead and contact forms as routes that write to workspace files, and replaces the premium integrations with direct connections you control. Templates, hosting, and the domain stay on the $19 plan.
dont_kill_if: A real store runs through Squarespace checkout, or gift cards and customer accounts are in use. Commerce through the site is a wall; do not migrate a working store to save $120.
build_brief: Goal: drop my Squarespace plan from Core to Basic without losing what Core added. List which Core features the site actually used in the last quarter: analytics depth, premium integrations, customer accounts, commerce volume. Rebuild the analytics as a weekly report automation from site exports, move lead forms to routes on my Zo, and confirm nothing sold recently needs Core checkout terms. Downgrade at the end of the billing cycle. Done when the site runs on Basic for a full month with nothing missing.
